cve.title: CVE-2026-44983 - smallbitvec: Safe API Triggered Heap Buffer Overflow via Integer Overflow
cve.contentSnippet: smallbitvec is a growable bit-vector for Rust, optimized for size. From 1.0.1 to 2.6.0, an integer overflow in the internal capacity calculation of smallbitvec can lead to an undersized heap allocation, resulting in a heap buffer overflow through safe APIs only. This allows memor

cve.title: CVE-2026-44213 - OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Instana bypasses TLS certificate validation when a proxy is configured
cve.contentSnippet: The OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Instana exports telemetry to Instana backend. cve.title: CVE-2025-43451 - Apple macOS Tahoe Local File Inclusion
cve.contentSnippet: A permissions issue was addressed by removing the vulnerable code. This issue is fixed in macOS Tahoe 26. An app may be able to access sensitive user data.

cve.title: CVE-2025-46307 - "Apple macOS Tahoe Data Access Vulnerability"
cve.contentSnippet: A logic issue was addressed with improved restrictions. This issue is fixed in macOS Tahoe 26. An app may be able to access sensitive user data.

cve.title: CVE-2025-46280 - Apple macOS Tahoe Memory Corruption
cve.contentSnippet: An out-of-bounds read was addressed with improved bounds checking. This issue is fixed in macOS Tahoe 26. An app may be able to cause unexpected system termination.

cve.title: CVE-2025-43289 - Apple macOS Malicious App Data Access Vulnerability
cve.contentSnippet: A logic issue was addressed with improved validation. This issue is fixed in macOS Sequoia 15.7, macOS Sonoma 14.8, macOS Tahoe 26. A malicious app may be able to access sensitive user data.

cve.title: CVE-2025-43290 - Apple macOS File System Permissions Vulnerability
cve.contentSnippet: A permissions issue was addressed with additional restrictions. This issue is fixed in macOS Sequoia 15.7, macOS Sonoma 14.8, macOS Tahoe 26. An app may be able to modify protected parts of the file system.

cve.title: CVE-2025-46284 - Apple macOS Root Privilege Escalation
cve.contentSnippet: A race condition was addressed with additional validation. This issue is fixed in macOS Sequoia 15.7, macOS Tahoe 26. An app may be able to gain root privileges.

cve.title: CVE-2025-43306 - Apple macOS Root Privilege Escalation Vulnerability
cve.contentSnippet: A logic issue was addressed with improved checks. This issue is fixed in macOS Sequoia 15.7, macOS Sonoma 14.8, macOS Tahoe 26. A malicious app may be able to gain root privileges.

cve.title: CVE-2026-44788 - SharpCompress: Directory traversal via directory entries in WriteToDirectory (zip slip variant)
cve.contentSnippet: SharpCompress is a fully managed C# library to deal with many compression types and formats. cve.title: CVE-2026-42015 - Gnutls: gnutls: memory corruption due to off-by-one error in pkcs#12 bag handling
cve.contentSnippet: A flaw was found in gnutls. An off-by-one error exists in the PKCS#12 bag element bounds check. This vulnerability allows an remote attacker to write past the internal array of a PKCS#12 bag when appending to a bag that already contains 32 elements. This memory corruption could l

cve.title: CVE-2026-42013 - Gnutls: gnutls: certificate validation bypass due to oversized subject alternative name
cve.contentSnippet: A flaw was found in gnutls. When validating certificates, an oversized Subject Alternative Name (SAN) could cause the validation process to incorrectly fall back to checking the Common Name (CN) field. This could allow a remote attacker to bypass proper certificate validation, po

cve.title: CVE-2026-42012 - Gnutls: gnutls: certificate validation bypass due to improper handling of uri and srv sans
cve.contentSnippet: A flaw was found in gnutls. A remote attacker could exploit this vulnerability by presenting a specially crafted certificate that contains Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) or Service (SRV) Subject Alternative Names (SANs). This could cause the certificate validation process to i

cve.title: CVE-2026-5260 - Gnutls: gnutls: information disclosure via heap overread in rsa key exchange
cve.contentSnippet: A flaw was found in libgnutls. A remote attacker, by sending an extremely short premaster secret during an RSA key exchange to a server using an RSA key backed by a PKCS#11 token, could trigger a short heap overread. This memory corruption vulnerability could lead to information
